differential_diagnoses: Intracranial hypertension due to hypervitaminosis A or medications
next_steps: Urgent magnetic resonance venography (MRV) and contrast-enhanced MRI of the brain
Lumbar puncture with opening pressure measurement and cerebrospinal fluid analysis
Ophthalmologic examination for papilledema or evidence of optic nerve involvement
ESR and CRP testing to evaluate for giant cell arteritis
Discontinue terlipressin and assess for potential side effects contributing to headache

differential_diagnoses: Terlipressin-associated adverse effect (hypertensive crisis or vasoconstriction)
next_steps: Discontinue terlipressin immediately and monitor blood pressure
Urgent magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and magnetic resonance venography (MRV) of the brain
Ophthalmologic examination for papilledema or visual field deficits
Check coagulation studies (PT/INR, platelets) and fibrinogen
